title: Google Ads attribution & conversion schema (Slice 1)
description: Contracts for the ad measurement foundation. ad_attribution is keyed by leadId and holds click context only (never PII). ads_conversion_events is the deduplicated conversion ladder, one event per (leadId, type). Implemented by js/intelligence/ad-attribution.js and js/revenue/ads-conversion-ledger.js.

leadId.description: Join key to the Lead OS record. The attribution collection carries NO name/phone/email/address.

gclid.description: Google click ID — the offline-conversion join key.

landingPage.description: Path only, no query string with identifiers.

channel.description: How the lead arrived — not who it is.

city.description: Coarse geo for service-area analysis; never a street address.

consent.description: First-party-data consent state. Only 'granted' records are eligible for conversion upload.

conversion.description: Legacy single-conversion record (ad-attribution.js recordConversion). The full ladder lives in ads_conversion_events.

id.description: '<leadId>:<type>' — the dedupe key. One event per lead per type, ever.

primarySignal.description: true only for JOB_WON / JOB_COMPLETED / HIGH_MARGIN_JOB. LEAD_CREATED is a volume signal and must never be treated as revenue truth.

valueUSD.description: Realized value where knowable. Never estimated.

sourceRef.description: The quoteId/jobId/invoiceId that grounds this event.

OfflineConversionPayload.description: Google-ready upload payload emitted by uploadQueue(). Generated only for bidding-eligible events whose lead has a click id AND consent === 'granted'. Generation and transmission are separate governed steps — nothing in this codebase calls the Google Ads API.

orderId.description: Mirrors the event id so Google-side dedupe matches ours.
